title: Nursing Home Respite Care
text:
Nursing Home Respite Care - SupportGoWhere
Nursing Home Respite Care
Share link
A stay-in, subsidised care option with healthcare services for seniors with high care needs if caregivers need a short break on an ad hoc basis during the day.
SERVICE HIGHLIGHTS
Fees payable
Referral required
Collapse all sections
About this support

Short term stay-in care for seniors who are physically or mentally disabled due to illness, and rely on caregivers for daily care needs.

Who is this for?

For seniors

Need help in most of their activities of daily living and/or have daily nursing care needs

Has stable medical condition

Does not need urgent medical attention

Singapore Citizen (SC), Permanent Resident (PR) or foreigner

For family or caregivers

Looking for short-term respite when they want to take a break from caregiver roles, or

Caregivers are not available, e.g. when foreign domestic helper goes on home leave

What to expect?

You need to prepare the latest medical report/doctor memo/hospital discharge summary of your loved one.

The medical report should include the following details:

Diagnosis

Clinical history

Current functional status

Cognitive and behavioural symptoms (if applicable)

Drug allergy history and current list of medications

Type of nursing procedures required and due date for change

Chest X-Ray report

COVID-19 vaccination history

Supporting documents (e.g. helper's air tickets)

The medical report/discharge summary and chest X-Ray results, should dated no earlier than 6 months from the intended date of admission.

Additional documents may be required upon request to facilitate the application.

There must be a discharge care plan for your loved one at the end of the respite care period.

Subsidies are available depending on the means-test result.

Only applicable to SC and PR

Applicants for care services may need to fulfil the services’ eligibility criteria and providers’ service boundaries, if any, and enrolments are subject to availability of vacancies.

Preparing earlier will ensure that you have sufficient time to work out the necessary arrangements with your preferred centre

How to apply?

You will need to get a referral from a hospital, polyclinic or GP who is familiar with your loved ones’ condition and needs.

You may also contact the service provider for a discussion. Visit AIC’s website to search for service providers by postal code, address or your device’s current location.

For further assistance, contact the Agency for Integrated Care (AIC) at 1800 650 6060, email enquiries@aic.sg or walk in to a nearby AIC Link.

Information last updated on 04 Mar 2026
